folio.label: 31r
folio.l: And hast in euäy regne , and euäy lond
Of armes , alle the brydeles in æin hond
And hem fortunest , as the list deuyse
Accepte of me , my pitous sacrifise
If so be , that my ©outhe , may deserue
And æ my mygút , be worthy for to êue
Thyn godhed , that I may be on of thyne
Than preye I the , to rewe vp on my pyne
For thilk pyne , and thilk hote fyre
In whicú , æè whilom , brendest for desire
Whan that æè vsedest , the beaute
Of fair ©ong and fressú , venus fre
And haddest hire in armes , at thy wylle
Al thougú the ones , on a tyme mys felle
Whan vulcanus , had caugút the in his laas
And fond the liggynge , by his wyf , Allas
For thilk sorwe , that was tho in thyn hert
Haue reuthe as wel , vp on my peynes smert
I am ©ong , and vnkonyng , as æè wost
And as I trowe , with loue offendestoffendedoffendestd most
That euäe was ony , lyues creature
For she æat doth me , al this woo endure
Ne reccheth neuäe , wheiæä I synke or flete
And wel I wot , or she me mäcy hete
I mot w strengthe , wynne hire in the place
And wel I wot , with outen help & grace
Of the , ne may my strengthe , nat a vaille
Than help me lord , to morwe in my bataille
For thilk fire , that whilom brent the
As wele as thilk fire , now brenneth me
And do æ I to morwe , may han victorie
Myn be the trauaylle , & thyn be the glorie
Thy souäeygne temple , wyl I most honoåe
Of ony place , and alwey most laboåe
In thy plesaunce , and in thy craftes strong
And in thy temple , I wol my baner hong
And alle the armes , of my companye
And euäe mo , vn to that day I dye
Eterne fire , I wyl byfore the fynde
And eke to this a vow , I wyl me bynde
My berd myn her , æ hangeth long a doun
That neuäe ©et felte , non offensiou~
Of Rasour ne of Shere , I wyl the ©eue
And ben thyn trewe serunt , whyl I lyue
